blocks:
Ayahs

وَيْلٌ لِّلْمُطَفِّفِينَ (۱)
Woe to the defrauders, who use short measures, (1)
ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ٱكْتَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ (۲)
who, when they measure [a commodity bought] from the people, take the full measure, (2)
وَإِذَا كَالُوهُمْ أَو وَّزَنُوهُمْ يُخْسِرُونَ (۳)
but diminish when they measure or weigh for them. (3)
أَلَا يَظُنُّ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَنَّهُم مَّبْعُوثُونَ (٤)
Do they not know that they will be resurrected (4)
لِيَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (٥)
on a tremendous day, (5)
يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلنَّاسُ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٦)
a day when mankind will stand before the Lord of all the worlds? (6)

Key Points

"Wayl" (وَيْل) means destruction and torment. Based on narrations, it is the name of a valley in Hell or one of its gates. "Tafīf" (طفیف) and qalīl (قلیل) have the same meaning. "Taṭfīf" (تطفیف) means reducing or shortchanging, and its root is from ṭaff (طفّ), meaning 'side' or 'edge'. A seller who skims from the edge of the goods is called a muṭaffif (مطفّف) (shortchanger). Ibn Mas'ūd narrates that anyone who shortchanges his prayer is included under this Ayah. (7) Shortchanging in every profession is commensurate with that profession: a teacher, a worker, a writer, a seller, an engineer, a doctor, and other professions—if any of them fall short in performing their duty, they are shortchangers. The shortchanger considers himself clever, does not love people, does not recognize Allah as the Provider (Rāziq), and commits economic injustice against society. Shortchanging is among the major sins (gunāhān-e kabīrah) because the promise of torment has been issued for it. The possibility of shortchanging or the sensitivity in measuring by volume (kayl) is greater than by weight (wazn). Therefore, in one Ayah, only volume is mentioned, and in the Ayah after it, volume is mentioned again first, followed by weight. We read in narrations: Whenever people become afflicted with the sin of shortchanging, the earth withholds its produce from them, and the people are threatened with famine. (8) Economic discipline and precision are at the forefront of the reform programs of the Prophets (Anbiyā'). Just as Prophet Shu'ayb said to his people: «اوفوا الكیل و لا تكونوا من المخسرین وزنوا بالقسطاس المستقیم و لا تبخسوا الناس اشیاءهم و لا تعثوا فى الارض مفسدین» (9) Give the measure (in transaction) fully and completely, and do not be among those who reduce or cause loss. Weigh with the correct balance, do not shortchange people's goods, and do not act corruptly upon the earth.

7) Tafsir Namūneh. 8) Tafsir Al-Mīzān. 9) Shu'arā, 181 - 183.

Messages

1- Announce the danger using warning words. «ویل للمطفّفین»

2- Unlawful wealth (māl harām) gained through shortchanging is a factor in man's downfall. «ویل للمطفّفین»

3- Making a difference between taking and giving is shortchanging. Do not wish for others what you do not wish for yourself. «اكتالو... یستوفون - كالوهم... یخسرون»

4- Shortchanging anyone, even if they are not Muslim, is wrong. «على الناس»

5- Worse than the sin itself is becoming accustomed to the sin, such that shortchanging becomes one's occupation. «یخسرون» (The imperfect verb form indicates continuity.)

6- To prevent economic corruption, the remembrance of the Resurrection (Ma'ād) must be kept alive in hearts. «الا یظنّ اولئك انّهم مبعوثون»

7- Belief in the Resurrection, even if only at the level of conjecture, is a deterrent. «الا یظنّ اولئك انّهم مبعوثون»

8- The events of the Day of Judgment are great and terrifying. «لیومٍ عظیم»

9- We must be accountable for shortchanging and for the rights of the people (Haqq an-Nās). «یقوم النّاس لربّ العالمین»

10- The retribution and punishment of criminals are among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «یقوم النّاس لربّ العالمین»

Explanations

Portrait of Sūrat Al-Muṭaffifīn

This Sūrah has thirty-six Ayahs and was revealed in Mecca, although based on some narrations, its initial Ayahs are Medinan. The name of the Sūrah is taken from the first Ayah and means 'The Shortchangers'. This Sūrah begins with a warning to the shortchangers and identifies the root cause of this behavior as a lack of belief in the Day of Judgment. The story of the wicked, sinful, and transgressing people, and their reaction to the Divine verses—which indicates the rust that has covered their hearts—forms another part of the Ayahs of this Sūrah. Of course, the description of the eternal blessings and the joy and delight of the people of Paradise illuminates the light of hope in the hearts of the believers and comforts them against the stings and taunts of the criminals, so that they do not become saddened by their humiliation and mockery. Based on narrations, reciting this Sūrah in prayer brings security from torment on the Day of Judgment, and Allah will quench his thirst with the pure drinks (ṭahūr) of Paradise. (6)

6) Tafsir Nūr ath-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتَـٰبَ ٱلْفُجَّارِ لَفِى سِجِّينٍ (۷)
Indeed, the record of the vicious is in Sijjeen. (7)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا سِجِّينٌ (۸)
And what will show you what is Sijjeen? (8)
كِتَـٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ (۹)
It is a written record. (9)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۱٠)
Woe to the deniers on that day, (10)
ٱلَّذِينَ يُكَذِّبُونَ بِيَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (۱۱)
who deny the Day of Retribution; (11)
وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ (۱۲)
and none denies it except every sinful transgressor. (12)
إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَـٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۳)
When Our signs are recited to him, he says, ‘Myths of the ancients!’ (13)

Key Points

"Sijjin" (سجّین) is derived from "Sijn" (سِجْن), meaning prison, and the pattern fi'īl (فِعّیل) is used for emphasis (hyperbole): an eternal and severe prison. "Marqūm" (مرقوم) is derived from "Raqm" (رقم), meaning a clear and distinct line, such that it cannot be confused with other writings. Therefore, "Kitābun Marqūm" (كتاب مرقوم) means that the destiny of the wicked (fājirūn) is completely clear and specified, and they are trapped in the eternal prison of Hellfire (Dūzakh). The intent of "Dīn" (دین) in the verses related to the Day of Judgment (Qiyāmah) is recompense (Jazā). "Yukadhdhibūna bi-Yawm al-Dīn" (یكذّبون بیوم الدّین) is, just like the verse in Sūrat al-Ḥamd, "Māliki Yawm al-Dīn" (مالك یوم الدین), meaning the Master of the Day of Recompense. "Athīm" (أثیم), derived from "Ithm" (اِثم), refers to someone who is deeply immersed in sin. The phrase "Waylun Yawma'idhin lil-Mukadhdhibīn" (ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین) can be interpreted in two ways: A: Woe to the short-changer whose destiny leads to the denial of the Day of Judgment. B: Woe to those who do not believe in the punishment of the short-changers on the Day of Judgment. "Asāṭīr" (اساطیر) is the plural of "Usṭūrah" (اسطورة), meaning legend or fable, which contains beautiful and captivating content but lacks reality and is woven from the minds and imaginations of storytellers. "Asāṭīr al-Awwalīn" (اساطیر الاولین) means that the ancients fabricated these legends and the Prophet is merely recounting them. In the history of Ashura, we read that Imam Husayn عليه السلام admonished Yazīd's army several times and extracted their admission: "Am I not the son of Fāṭimah, the daughter of your Prophet? Have I made lawful what is unlawful, or unlawful what is lawful? Did you not invite me with thousands of letters?" And so on... Finally, He said: «قد مُلئت بطونكم من الحرام» (Your bellies have been filled with the unlawful), and thus the word of truth no longer affects your souls. Today, none of the world's colleges of educational sciences or psychology mention the effect of lawful (ḥalāl) and unlawful (ḥarām) sustenance on human upbringing and soul, and scholars in the East and West of the world are oblivious to the effect of nutrition on character development.

Messages

1- The short-changer is wicked (fājir) and tears the veil of piety. "Lil-muṭaffifīn... al-fujjār" (للمطفّفین... الفجّار)

2- The destiny of the wicked is certain and specified. "Kitābun Marqūm" (كتاب مرقوم)

3- Sin leads man toward disbelief (kufr) and the denial of truths. "Wa mā yukadhdhibu bihi illā kullu mu‘tadin athīm" (و ما یكذّب به الاّ كُل معتد اَثیم)

4- Those who call the verses of the Quran legends and fables are those who are immersed in the whirlpool of sin and transgression. "Kullu mu‘tadin athīm idhā tutlā ‘alayhi āyātunā qāla asāṭīru al-awwalīn" (كُلّ معتد اَثیم اذا تتلى علیه آیاتنا قال اساطیر الاولین)

5- Justifying revelation as a fable is a way to remain a disbeliever. "Qāla asāṭīru al-awwalīn" (قال اساطیر الاولین)

6- Unlawful sustenance (ḥarām luqmah) pushes man to the brink of disbelief. (At the beginning of the Sūrah, the discussion was about short-changing, which is a means of acquiring unlawful sustenance; in the subsequent verses, the discussion turned to wickedness, and then to denial.) "Waylun Yawma'idhin lil-Mukadhdhibīn" (ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین)

7- Human behavior affects his beliefs and convictions. Continuous transgression and sin lead man to denial. "Wa mā yukadhdhibu bihi illā kullu mu‘tadin athīm" (و ما یكذّب به الاّ كلّ معتد اثیم)

8- One should not be content with a single reminder or warning; remembrance and recitation must be continuous. "Tutlā ‘alayhi āyātunā" (تُتلى علیه آیاتنا) (The imperfect verb indicates continuity.)

9- The Quran is highly captivating, even to opponents. "Qāla asāṭīru al-awwalīn" (قال اساطیر الاولین) (A legend/fable [usṭūrah] refers to beautiful and highly captivating stories.)

Ayahs

كَلَّا ۖ بَلْ ۜ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۱٤)
No, that is not the case! Rather, their hearts have been sullied by what they have been earning. (14)
كَلَّآ إِنَّهُمْ عَن رَّبِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَّمَحْجُوبُونَ (۱٥)
Indeed, they will be alienated from their Lord on that day. (15)
ثُمَّ إِنَّهُمْ لَصَالُوا۟ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۱٦)
Afterward they will enter hell, (16)
ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ (۱۷)
then told, ‘This is what you used to deny!’ (17)

Key Points

«رَین» means rust or tarnish that settles upon valuable objects. Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: Whenever sin overcomes a person, there will be no hope for his salvation, and this is the meaning of «كلا بل ران على قلوبهم» (10). Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: There is no servant except that his heart is white. With the commission of every sin, a black spot appears in that whiteness. If he repents, that blackness is erased, but if he insists on sin, the blackness expands and covers all the whiteness of the heart, and the person will no longer return to goodness and felicity (11). Imam Ridha عليه السلام said: The meaning of «انّهم عن ربّهم یومئذ لمحجوبون» is that they are deprived of Allah's reward, otherwise, Allah has no location (12).

10) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 11) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 12) Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 162.

Messages

1- Considering the Quran a myth stems from the impurity of the heart. «كلاّ بل ران على قلوبهم»

2- Sin transforms the human soul and prevents it from perceiving the truth. «بل ران على قلوبهم ما كانوا یكسبون»

3- Human nature (Fitrah) is sound; what covers it is sin. «ران على قلوبهم»

4- In the culture of the Quran, the heart and soul are the center for perceiving realities. «ران على قلوبهم»

5- Insistence and persistence in sin cause the heart's tarnish. «ما كانوا یكسبون»

6- Action is the capital of man, through which he earns good deeds or bad deeds. «كانوا یكسبون»

7- The tarnish of sin, which prevents the perception of truth in this world, also prevents the reception of Divine favors in the Hereafter. «انهم عن ربّهم یومئذ لمحجوبون»

8- The guardians of Hell rebuke the inhabitants of Hell. «ثم یقال هذا الّذى كنتم به تكذّبون»

Ayahs

كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتَـٰبَ ٱلْأَبْرَارِ لَفِى عِلِّيِّينَ (۱۸)
Indeed, the record of the pious is in Illeeyun. (18)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا عِلِّيُّونَ (۱۹)
And what will show you what is Illeeyun? (19)
كِتَـٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ (۲٠)
It is a written record, (20)
يَشْهَدُهُ ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ (۲۱)
witnessed by those brought near [to Allah]. (21)
إِنَّ ٱلْأَبْرَارَ لَفِى نَعِيمٍ (۲۲)
The pious shall be amid bliss, (22)
عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ يَنظُرُونَ (۲۳)
observing, [as they recline] on couches. (23)
تَعْرِفُ فِى وُجُوهِهِمْ نَضْرَةَ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (۲٤)
You will perceive in their faces the freshness of bliss. (24)
يُسْقَوْنَ مِن رَّحِيقٍ مَّخْتُومٍ (۲٥)
They will be served with a sealed pure wine, (25)
خِتَـٰمُهُۥ مِسْكٌ ۚ وَفِى ذَٰلِكَ فَلْيَتَنَافَسِ ٱلْمُتَنَـٰفِسُونَ (۲٦)
whose seal is musk—for such let the viers vie— (26)
وَمِزَاجُهُۥ مِن تَسْنِيمٍ (۲۷)
and whose seasoning is from Tasneem, (27)
عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ (۲۸)
a spring where those brought near [to Allah] drink. (28)

Key Points

‘Illiyyīn (عِلّیّین) is the plural of ‘Illī (عِلِّى), meaning high ranks and levels extending infinitely. The intent of the Book of the Righteous (Kitāb al-Abrār) is the destiny of the good people on the Day of Judgment, and marqūm (مرقوم) means specified and marked in such a way that it cannot be confused with other writings. Arā’ik (ارائك) is the plural of arīkah (اریكة), meaning a couch or throne that is decorated and prepared for reclining. Naḍrah (نَضرة) means beauty and freshness, na‘īm (نعیم) means abundant blessing, and raḥīq (رَحیق) means pure wine. Tanāfus (تنافس) is either derived from nafīs (precious), meaning two people strive to obtain a precious object, or it is derived from nafas (breath/soul), meaning two people exert their last breath and effort to obtain something.(13)

Hospitality in Paradise has ranks and levels: for one group, it is «یسقون» (they are given to drink), and for another group, it is «سقاهم ربّهم» (their Lord gives them to drink)(14)—meaning some receive favors through an intermediary, and some receive them directly. Some have said that the intent of musk (misk) in «ختامه مسك» (its seal is musk) is the sealed vessel of wine. The Near Ones (Muqarrabūn) drink from Tasnīm, but the Righteous (Abrār) will drink from a beverage mixed with Tasnīm.

The beverages of Paradise are of various types: some flow in rivers: «انهار من ماء، انهار من لبن، انهار من خمر، انهار من عسل» (rivers of water, rivers of milk, rivers of wine, rivers of honey)(15); some are in sealed vessels: «ختامه مسك» (its seal is musk); and some pour down from the sky of Paradise or from its upper layers, which is Tasnīm.(16) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Tasnīm is the best drink in Paradise, and it is exclusive to the Prophet and his Household, pouring down from above and flowing through their dwellings.(17)

13) Tafsir Rahnama. 14) Insan, 21. 15) Muhammad, 15. 16) Tafsir Namuneh. 17) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Allah’s promises regarding the reward of the good people are certain. «ان الابرار لفى نعیم» (The word inna, the letter lām, and the nominal sentence structure indicate the certainty of the promise.)

2- Receiving blessings in the presence of others multiplies the pleasure. «یشهده المقرّبون»

3- A high position and overlooking beautiful vistas are among the blessings of the people of Paradise. «على الارائك ینظرون»

4- A person’s spiritual and psychological states affect his body. «تعرف فى وجوههم» (The radiance of the faces of the people of Paradise is visible to man.)

5- The pristine and untouched nature of the blessings of Paradise is a value. «مختوم»

6- The pleasures of Paradise have no side effects. «ختامه مسك»

7- In Islamic culture, the world is a field for competition and rivalry, specifically rivalry for obtaining the blessings of the Hereafter. «و فى ذلك فلیتنافس المتنافسون»

8- The people of Paradise have ranks and levels. «الابرار - المقربون»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ (۲۹)
Indeed the guilty used to laugh at the faithful, (29)
وَإِذَا مَرُّوا۟ بِهِمْ يَتَغَامَزُونَ (۳٠)
and when they passed them by they would wink at each other, (30)
وَإِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمُ ٱنقَلَبُوا۟ فَكِهِينَ (۳۱)
and when they returned to their folks they would return amused, (31)
وَإِذَا رَأَوْهُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَضَآلُّونَ (۳۲)
and when they saw them they would say, ‘Indeed those are the astray!’ (32)
وَمَآ أُرْسِلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ حَـٰفِظِينَ (۳۳)
Though they were not sent to watch over them. (33)
فَٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْكُفَّارِ يَضْحَكُونَ (۳٤)
So today the faithful will laugh at the faithless, (34)
عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ يَنظُرُونَ (۳٥)
observing from their couches: (35)
هَلْ ثُوِّبَ ٱلْكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ (۳٦)
Have the faithless been requited for what they used to do? (36)

Key Points

| Ghamz (غَمز) means finding fault with others through gestures of the eye and eyebrow.

Messages

1- Believers must be prepared for the unjust treatment from sinners and criminals. «انّ الّذین اجرموا»

2- Do not let the laughter, gestures, taunts, and sarcasm of others discourage you, because they themselves are criminals. «انّ الّذین اجرموا»

3- Mockery and humiliation are the long-standing methods of criminals. «كانوا... یضحكون»

4- The criminal is not satisfied with his own sin; he humiliates the pure and inflicts psychological harm upon them. «یضحكون، یتغامزون»

5- Creating satire and humor to mock believers is one of the methods used by criminals. «انقلبوا فكهین»

6- Persistence in sin changes a person's perspective and judgment. (It is interesting that the criminals consider the believers to be misguided.) «قالوا انّ هؤلاء لضالّون»

7- Sinners are not qualified to judge or express opinions about believers. «قالوا انّ هؤلاء لضالّون و ما ارسلوا علیهم حافظین»

8- Believers should not expect approval from their opponents. «و ما ارسلوا علیهم حافظین»

9- It is as if the criminals considered themselves the custodians of the believers, which Allah negates. «و ما ارسلوا علیهم حافظین»

10- On the Day of Judgment, believers will be the cause of psychological distress for the criminals, because there, the believers will also laugh at them. «فالیوم الّذین آمنوا من الكفّار یضحكون»

11- Punishment in the Hereafter corresponds to the sin committed in this world. (The punishment for mocking laughter in this world is being laughed at in the Hereafter.) «الّذین اجرموا كانوا... یضحكون - الّذین آمنوا... یضحكون»

12- The punishments of the Day of Judgment are the reflection of man's own deeds. «هل ثُوّب الكفّار ما كانوا یفعلون»
